agent2: I agree with agent1's conclusion. The mechanism behind this involves ascorbic acid’s ability to reduce Fe(III) to Fe(II). This reduction makes iron more soluble and accessible for intestinal uptake, thereby increasing its absorbability—especially important since non-heme iron from plant sources has lower bioavailability than heme iron from animal products. Furthermore, vitamin C also helps maintain an acidic environment in the stomach, which can further promote iron solubility.  
Answer: B

Vitamin C enhances non-heme iron absorption by converting insoluble ferric iron (Fe³⁺) into soluble ferrous iron (Fe²⁺), which is more readily absorbed in the small intestine.
